hero.description: Transforming aspiring students into global business leaders, entrepreneurs, and finance professionals through an industry-integrated curriculum and corporate exposure.

vision.title: Vision
vision.label: Our Purpose
vision.text: To be a globally recognized center of excellence in management education, developing ethical, innovative, and socially responsible leaders who can navigate the complexities of the modern corporate world.

mission.title: Mission
mission.label: Our Goal
points: Provide a dynamic learning environment that blends theoretical management principles with practical industry applications.
Foster entrepreneurial spirit through dedicated incubation centers and startup mentoring.
Develop strong partnerships with the corporate sector to ensure curriculum relevance and robust placement opportunities.
Imbue students with leadership qualities, ethical values, and global business perspectives.

bba.title: Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA)
bba.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream with minimum 50% marks from a recognized board
bba.description: The BBA program is designed to develop future corporate leaders and entrepreneurs by providing a strong foundation in core business, management, and financial principles.
bba.metaTitle: Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) | Commerce & Management | CPU
bba.metaDescription: Pursue a 3-year BBA degree at Career Point University. Master management, finance, marketing, and leadership with industry-driven projects.

hero.description: Step into the corporate world. Build essential skills in organizational behavior, corporate finance, and strategic marketing.

overview.subtitle: What is the BBA Programme?
paragraphs: The BBA programme at Career Point University is structured to transform ambitious students into capable business professionals. It bridges the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical corporate challenges through case studies, industrial visits, and role-playing.
Students develop a holistic understanding of how organizations function, preparing them for dynamic roles in banking, retail, consulting, and their own entrepreneurial ventures.

scope.body: A BBA degree is the perfect launchpad for a corporate career. Graduates are highly sought after for roles such as Financial Analyst, Business Development Executive, HR Generalist, and Marketing Coordinator. It also builds the perfect foundation for those looking to pursue an MBA or start their own business, offering insights into strategic planning, economics, and corporate law.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in the corporate world

curriculum.subtitle: A business curriculum designed for future managers
curriculum.introNote: The BBA curriculum focuses on core management subjects, enhanced by soft skills training and quantitative analysis.
curriculum.outroNote: Graduates complete a mandatory corporate internship, ensuring they are job-ready upon graduation.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The BBA program focuses on building a strong base in the first year, moving towards functional management in the second year, and specializing in the final year through electives and internships.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Principles of Management, Business Economics, Financial Accounting, Business Communication, Computer Applications in Business. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Organizational Behavior, Macro Economics, Cost Accounting, Business Mathematics, Environmental Science. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Human Resource Management, Marketing Management, Corporate Finance, Business Statistics, Soft Skills for Managers. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Research Methodology, Business Law, Operations Management, Consumer Behavior, Minor Research Project. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-5
accordionSections.content: Entrepreneurship Development, Strategic Management, Elective I (Finance/HR/Mktg), Elective II, Summer Internship Report. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-6
accordionSections.content: International Business, Business Ethics & Corporate Governance, Elective III, Elective IV, Final Business Project. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Departmental Elective
accordionSections.content: Elective options include: Digital Marketing, Investment Banking, Performance Management, Retail Management, and Financial Derivatives.

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the BBA programme

items.a: Candidates must have passed 10+2 from a recognized board with a minimum of 50% marks. Students from any stream (Arts/Commerce/Science) are eligible to apply.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for BBA admission?

items.a: BBA is a 3-year (6 semester) programme. The tuition fee is ₹20,000 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure of the BBA programme?

items.a: While B.Com focuses heavily on accounting, taxation, and financial numbers, BBA is a management degree focusing on leadership, marketing, HR, and overall business operations.
items.q: What is the difference between BBA and B.Com?

items.a: BBA students can choose electives in their final year in areas like Human Resource Management, Marketing, Finance, and International Business.
items.q: Are there specializations offered in BBA?

items.a: BBA graduates are hired as Management Trainees, Sales Executives, Financial Analysts, and HR Coordinators. We have strong tie-ups with banks, FMCG, and IT consulting firms.
items.q: What kind of placements can I expect after completing BBA?

items.a: Yes. Merit-based scholarships of up to 100% on tuition fees are available for students scoring above 90% in their 12th board exams.
items.q: Is there any scholarship available for BBA students?

items.a: Yes, a 4-6 week summer internship in a corporate organization is a mandatory part of the curriculum to ensure practical exposure.
items.q: Is an internship compulsory during the course?

items.a: Yes, BBA is considered the best foundational degree for an MBA. It gives you a significant head start in understanding core management subjects.
items.q: Does BBA help if I want to do an MBA later?

years.contentDesc: Focus on foundational business theories, economics, basic accounting, and communication skills.

Choose your electives, complete a corporate summer internship, and focus on strategic management.

bba-data-analytics.title: BBA (Data Analytics & Visualization)
bba-data-analytics.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream (with Basic Math/Stats preferred) with minimum 50% marks from a recognized board
bba-data-analytics.description: A cutting-edge BBA specialization designed to merge core business management principles with advanced data science, predictive modeling, and data visualization skills.
bba-data-analytics.metaTitle: BBA Data Analytics & Visualization | Commerce & Management | CPU
bba-data-analytics.metaDescription: Pursue a 3-year BBA in Data Analytics at Career Point University. Master Python, Tableau, SQL, and business intelligence for high-paying corporate roles.

hero.description: Learn to translate raw data into strategic business decisions. Master tools like Power BI, Tableau, and Python while building a strong management foundation.

overview.subtitle: What is the BBA Data Analytics Programme?
paragraphs: The BBA in Data Analytics & Visualization is a unique hybrid degree that bridges the gap between IT and Business. In today’s data-driven world, companies don't just need managers; they need leaders who can read data, spot trends, and forecast business outcomes.

This programme trains students extensively in data extraction, statistical analysis, and visual storytelling using industry-standard tools, alongside traditional management subjects like marketing, HR, and finance.

scope.body: Data is the new oil, and professionals who can refine it are in massive demand. Graduates of this specialization are hired as Business Analysts, Data Analysts, Marketing Strategists, and BI Consultants across sectors like E-commerce, FinTech, Healthcare, and IT. It also serves as the perfect foundation for pursuing a specialized MBA in Business Analytics or a Master's in Data Science.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in the data-driven corporate world

curriculum.subtitle: A hybrid curriculum blending Management with Data Science
curriculum.introNote: The curriculum is perfectly balanced: 50% core management principles and 50% intensive data analytics, programming, and visualization training.
curriculum.outroNote: Students complete a mandatory Capstone Data Project, solving real-world business problems using live datasets.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The BBA (Data Analytics) program begins with basic business and mathematical principles. By the second year, it heavily integrates programming and database management. The final year is completely focused on predictive analytics, machine learning for business, and interactive dashboards.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Principles of Management, Business Mathematics, Financial Accounting, Introduction to IT & Spreadsheets (Advanced Excel), Communication Skills. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Organizational Behavior, Business Statistics I, Macro Economics, Database Management Systems (SQL Basics), Environmental Science. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Marketing Management, Business Statistics II, Python for Data Analysis, Data Warehousing, Soft Skills. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Corporate Finance, Data Visualization using Tableau & Power BI, Research Methodology, E-Commerce & Digital Marketing. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-5
accordionSections.content: Predictive Analytics, Machine Learning for Business, Strategic Management, Elective I, Summer Internship Report. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-6
accordionSections.content: Big Data Analytics, Business Intelligence & Reporting, Elective II, Capstone Data Project. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Departmental Elective
accordionSections.content: Elective options include: Financial Analytics, Marketing Analytics (SEO/SEM Data), HR Analytics, and Supply Chain Analytics.

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the BBA Analytics programme

items.a: Candidates must have passed 10+2 from a recognized board with a minimum of 50% marks. Students from any stream are eligible, though having basic Mathematics/Statistics is advantageous.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for BBA (Data Analytics)?

items.a: It is a 3-year (6 semester) programme. The tuition fee is ₹25,000 per semester, plus a specialized Analytics Lab fee of ₹4,000 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure?

items.a: A regular BBA focuses purely on management theory. This specialization replaces some generic subjects with heavy IT and Data training, including Python, SQL, and Power BI.
items.q: How is this different from a regular BBA?

items.a: No prior coding experience is required. We start from the absolute basics of Python and SQL. However, a logical mindset and comfort with numbers will help.
items.q: Do I need to know coding before joining?

items.a: Graduates are hired as Business Analysts, Data Visualization Experts, MIS Analysts, and Junior Data Scientists in companies like TCS, Accenture, Amazon, and FinTech startups.
items.q: What kind of placements can I expect?

items.a: Yes. Merit-based scholarships of up to 100% on tuition fees are available for students scoring above 90% in their 12th board exams.
items.q: Is there any scholarship available?

items.a: Yes, completing a Capstone Project using real-world datasets and undergoing a corporate internship are mandatory to complete the degree.
items.q: Are there practical projects and internships?

items.a: Absolutely. It is the perfect undergraduate degree if you plan to pursue an MBA in Business Analytics, Information Systems, or a Master's in Data Science.
items.q: Does this help if I want to do an MBA in Analytics later?

years.contentDesc: Focus on foundational business theories, statistics, and advanced spreadsheet management.

Master visual storytelling with Tableau/Power BI and apply predictive models to real business problems.

list.name: Megha Nair
list.text: Learning Tableau and Power BI alongside Corporate Finance gave me a massive edge. I am now building financial dashboards for a top consulting firm.

list.name: Siddharth Jain
list.text: The Capstone project where we analyzed real e-commerce data was a game-changer. It became the highlight of my resume.

bcom.title: Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com.)
bcom.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream (Commerce preferred) with minimum 50% marks from a recognized board
bcom.description: A comprehensive undergraduate program focusing on financial accounting, taxation, corporate law, and auditing to build a robust career in the global financial sector.
bcom.metaTitle: Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com.) | Commerce & Management | CPU
bcom.metaDescription: Pursue a 3-year B.Com. degree at Career Point University. Master accounting, taxation, GST, and corporate finance. Ideal foundation for CA and CS aspirants.

hero.description: Master the language of business. Build essential skills in financial reporting, direct and indirect taxation, and statutory compliance.

overview.subtitle: What is the B.Com. Programme?
paragraphs: The B.Com. programme at Career Point University is meticulously structured to provide an in-depth understanding of the financial ecosystem. It goes beyond basic bookkeeping to encompass complex corporate accounting, auditing frameworks, and the ever-evolving landscape of taxation (Direct Taxes and GST).

With a curriculum tightly aligned with professional courses like Chartered Accountancy (CA) and Company Secretary (CS), students are perfectly positioned to tackle competitive financial examinations or step directly into high-demand roles in corporate finance teams, banking, and audit firms.

scope.body: Every business, from a local startup to a multinational conglomerate, requires commerce graduates to manage its finances and compliance. Graduates of the B.Com. program are heavily recruited for roles such as Tax Consultant, Financial Accountant, Auditor, Investment Analyst, and Banking Executive. Furthermore, it lays the strongest possible academic foundation for pursuing prestigious professional qualifications like CA, CS, CMA, or academic progressions like M.Com and MBA in Finance.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in the core financial sector

curriculum.subtitle: A robust curriculum blending Accounting with Corporate Compliance
curriculum.introNote: The B.Com curriculum is heavily focused on numerical accuracy, financial laws, and modern accounting software training.
curriculum.outroNote: Students undergo practical training in software like Tally Prime to ensure absolute industry readiness upon graduation.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The B.Com program begins with foundational accounting and economics. The second year introduces corporate laws and direct taxation. The final year focuses on advanced auditing, indirect taxes (GST), and financial management, ensuring complete mastery of commercial operations.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Financial Accounting I, Micro Economics, Business Organization & Management, Business Mathematics, English Communication. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Financial Accounting II, Macro Economics, Business Law, Business Statistics, Environmental Science. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Corporate Accounting, Company Law, Income Tax Law and Practice, Indian Economy, E-Commerce Basics. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Cost Accounting, Business Communication, Indirect Taxes (GST & Customs), Entrepreneurship, Computerized Accounting (Tally). Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-5
accordionSections.content: Auditing and Corporate Governance, Financial Management, Elective I (Banking/Insurance), Elective II, Summer Internship Report. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-6
accordionSections.content: Management Accounting, International Business, Elective III, Elective IV, Final Commerce Project. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Departmental Elective
accordionSections.content: Elective options include: Indian Depository System, Principles of Marketing, Financial Markets & Institutions, and Corporate Tax Planning.

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the B.Com. programme

items.a: Candidates must have passed 10+2 from a recognized board with a minimum of 50% marks. While Commerce stream students are preferred, Science and Arts students with an aptitude for numbers can also apply.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for B.Com admission?

items.a: B.Com is a 3-year (6 semester) programme. The tuition fee is ₹15,000 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure of the B.Com programme?

items.a: B.Com is deeply focused on accounting, taxation, corporate law, and auditing. BBA is a management degree focusing on leadership, marketing, HR, and overall business operations. If you love numbers and finance, B.Com is the better choice.
items.q: What is the exact difference between B.Com and BBA?

items.a: Yes! Our B.Com syllabus is designed to heavily overlap with the foundational modules of CA and CS, making it the perfect graduation degree for aspirants of these professional courses.
items.q: Does this course help if I am preparing for CA or CS?

items.a: B.Com graduates are hired as Junior Accountants, Tax Consultants, Audit Assistants, and Banking Executives. Top recruiters include HDFC Bank, EY, KPMG, and various local corporate audit firms.
items.q: What kind of placements can I expect after completing B.Com?

items.a: Yes. Merit-based scholarships of up to 100% on tuition fees are available for students scoring above 90% in their 12th board exams.
items.q: Is there any scholarship available for B.Com students?

items.a: Yes, we provide hands-on training in tools like Tally Prime, Advanced Excel, and basic GST filing portals through our specialized commerce labs.
items.q: Will I learn accounting software like Tally?

items.a: Yes, B.Com is the traditional and most recognized pre-requisite for pursuing a Master of Commerce (M.Com) or an MBA in Finance.
items.q: Can I pursue an M.Com or MBA after this?

years.contentDesc: Master the golden rules of accounting, prepare financial statements, and understand micro-economic theories.
Dive deep into corporate structures, learn to calculate direct income taxes, and use digital accounting tools.
Master the complexities of indirect taxation (GST), learn how to audit corporate books, and understand financial management.

bha.title: Bachelor of Hospital Administration (BHA)
bha.eligibility: 10+2 in any stream (Science/Biology preferred but not mandatory) with minimum 50% marks
bha.description: A specialized management degree designed to train professionals in the administrative, operational, and financial functioning of hospitals and healthcare institutions.
bha.metaTitle: Bachelor of Hospital Administration (BHA) | Healthcare Management | CPU
bha.metaDescription: Pursue a 3-year BHA degree at Career Point University. Master clinical operations, health economics, and hospital management. High demand in the healthcare sector.

hero.description: Lead the healthcare revolution. Learn to manage complex hospital operations, patient care systems, and healthcare quality standards.

overview.subtitle: What is the BHA Programme?
paragraphs: The Bachelor of Hospital Administration (BHA) program bridges the critical gap between medical professionals and business management. It trains students to run the complex machinery of modern healthcare facilities, from multi-specialty hospitals to nursing homes and diagnostic centers.

The curriculum provides a deep dive into medical terminology, healthcare laws, hospital planning, and quality accreditations (like NABH and JCI), ensuring graduates can seamlessly manage doctors, patients, and hospital finances.

scope.body: The healthcare industry is one of the fastest-growing sectors globally, and there is a massive shortage of professionally trained hospital administrators. BHA graduates are urgently required as Operations Executives, Patient Care Coordinators, Health Insurance Managers, and Quality Assurance Officers. It is also the most logical and solid foundation for pursuing a Master of Hospital Administration (MHA) or an MBA in Healthcare Management.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in the booming healthcare sector

curriculum.subtitle: A specialized curriculum blending Management with Medical Operations
curriculum.introNote: The BHA curriculum introduces basic medical terminologies alongside core management principles tailored for the healthcare industry.
curriculum.outroNote: Students complete mandatory internships in reputed hospitals to gain real-world exposure to clinical and non-clinical administration.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The BHA program starts with basic management and introductory medical concepts. The second year focuses on specific hospital departments (OPD, IPD, Emergency). The final year is dedicated to healthcare laws, quality accreditations, and an intensive hospital internship.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Principles of Management, Introduction to Healthcare Delivery Systems, Medical Terminology I, Business Communication, Fundamentals of Accounting. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Hospital Operations Management (Clinical), Medical Terminology II, Health Economics, Organizational Behavior, Environmental Science. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Hospital Operations Management (Supportive Services), Human Resource Management in Healthcare, Epidemiology & Public Health, Hospital Planning. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Healthcare Laws and Ethics, Quality Management & Patient Safety (NABH), Material & Supply Chain Management in Hospitals, Health Insurance. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-5
accordionSections.content: Hospital Information Systems (HIS), Marketing of Healthcare Services, Disaster Management in Hospitals, Summer Internship Report. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-6
accordionSections.content: Strategic Management in Healthcare, Medical Tourism, Patient Care Management, Final Hospital Dissertation. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Departmental Elective
accordionSections.content: Elective options include: Health Analytics, NGOs and Public Health Administration, and Clinical Research Management.

Tuition and fees may be net once your scholarship criteria is evaluated.
A 50% scholarship is given to students clearing the University Aptitude Test with distinction.
If a student fails to maintain the required CGPA, they may lose scholarship eligibility.

reasons.desc: The healthcare sector is growing exponentially, and hospitals urgently need trained administrators to manage daily operations, ensuring a recession-proof career.
reasons.title: Recession-Proof Industry

reasons.desc: Gain hands-on clinical operations experience through mandatory internships in reputed multi-specialty hospitals and diagnostic centers.
reasons.title: Hospital Internships

reasons.desc: Learn critical medical terminologies and laws, allowing you to effectively bridge the communication gap between doctors and management.
reasons.title: Bridge Medicine & Business

reasons.desc: Receive specialized training in NABH and JCI accreditation processes, a highly demanded skill in modern healthcare quality assurance.
reasons.title: Quality Accreditation Training

reasons.desc: A BHA degree is the most direct and recognized pathway for pursuing a Master of Hospital Administration (MHA) program.
reasons.title: Pathway to MHA

whyJoin.subtitle: Unlock your potential with a Healthcare Management degree

features.desc: Curriculum heavily integrates training on modern Hospital Information Systems (HIS) used in multi-specialty hospitals.
features.title: Health IT Training

features.desc: Learn directly from senior medical superintendents, quality managers, and healthcare HR professionals.
features.title: Industry-Expert Faculty

features.desc: Regular field visits to blood banks, medical waste management plants, and hospital supportive service departments.
features.title: Clinical Field Visits

features.desc: Dedicated placement cell with strong ties to corporate hospital chains like Apollo, Fortis, and Max Healthcare.
features.title: Corporate Hospital Placements

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the BHA programme

items.a: Candidates must have passed 10+2 from a recognized board with a minimum of 50% marks. While Science/Biology students have a slight advantage in understanding medical terms, students from Commerce and Arts streams are equally eligible.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for BHA admission? Do I need Biology?

items.a: BHA is a 3-year (6 semester) programme. The tuition fee is ₹22,000 per semester, plus a clinical training fee of ₹3,500 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure of the BHA programme?

items.a: BBA is a general management degree for corporate offices. BHA is highly specialized for hospitals, teaching you medical terminology, patient care management, and healthcare laws alongside standard management principles.
items.q: How is BHA different from a regular BBA?

items.a: BHA graduates are hired as Hospital Operations Executives, Patient Care Managers, Quality Assurance (NABH) Coordinators, and Health Insurance Claims Managers in corporate hospitals and TPA firms.
items.q: What kind of jobs will I get after BHA?

items.a: Yes. Merit-based scholarships of up to 100% on tuition fees are available for students scoring above 90% in their 12th board exams.
items.q: Is there any scholarship available for BHA students?

items.a: Yes, completing a clinical internship in a functioning hospital is a mandatory part of the curriculum. The university assists in placing students in reputed hospitals for this training.
items.q: Is an internship in a hospital compulsory?

items.a: No, BHA is an administrative degree. You will learn to manage the hospital, handle patient flow, and manage doctors, but you will not be providing medical treatments or prescribing medicines.
items.q: Will I be treating patients as a BHA graduate?

items.a: Yes, BHA is the perfect undergraduate foundation for pursuing a Master of Hospital Administration (MHA) or an MBA in Healthcare Management.
items.q: Can I pursue an MHA or MBA after this?

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the BHA programme

roadmap.subtitle: Your journey from basic management to hospital administration.

years.contentDesc: Master fundamental management theories while learning the basics of human anatomy and medical terminology.
Dive deep into how clinical (OPD/IPD) and supportive services function, alongside healthcare laws and ethics.
Master quality standards like NABH, learn Hospital Information Systems, and complete a long-term hospital internship.

list.name: Neha Agarwal
list.text: The mandatory internships in the BHA program were a game-changer. They gave me the practical exposure I needed to secure a job at Apollo right after graduation.

list.name: Saurabh Mehra
list.text: The deep focus on NABH and patient safety in the final year directly aligned with my current role. The faculty's industry experience was incredibly helpful.

list.name: Ananya Desai
list.text: Learning medical terminology alongside business management gave me a unique edge in the health insurance sector. I am able to bridge the gap between doctors and corporate clients.

mba.title: Master of Business Administration (MBA)
mba.eligibility: Graduation in any discipline with minimum 50% marks from a recognized university
mba.description: A premier postgraduate program designed to build strategic thinkers, visionary leaders, and elite corporate professionals with deep expertise in HR, Marketing, or Finance.
mba.metaTitle: Master of Business Administration (MBA) | Commerce & Management | CPU
mba.metaDescription: Pursue a 2-year MBA degree at Career Point University. Specialize in HR, Marketing, or Finance. High-end corporate placements, live case studies, and expert faculty.

hero.description: Accelerate your career trajectory. Master the art of corporate strategy, financial modeling, and brand building through immersive, case-study-driven learning.

overview.subtitle: What is the MBA Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Business Administration (MBA) is the flagship program of the School of Commerce & Management. Designed for ambitious graduates, this course transforms raw talent into polished corporate executives capable of taking on leadership roles in Fortune 500 companies.

Our pedagogy moves away from rote learning, relying heavily on the Harvard Case Study methodology, live industry projects, corporate internships, and simulation labs. Students can choose to specialize in Human Resources, Marketing, or Finance, gaining deep, actionable expertise in their chosen domain.

scope.body: An MBA is the ultimate catalyst for career growth and salary multiplication. Graduates are aggressively recruited for mid-to-senior management roles such as Brand Managers, Investment Bankers, Talent Acquisition Heads, Management Consultants, and Business Development Directors. The entrepreneurial training embedded in the program also provides the perfect launchpad for students wanting to pitch to VCs and start their own disruptive ventures.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in the global corporate landscape

curriculum.subtitle: An elite curriculum designed for corporate dominance
curriculum.introNote: The MBA curriculum is divided into a rigorous foundation year followed by a highly specialized second year tailored to the student's chosen major.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory 8-week corporate summer internship and a final Capstone Project ensure that students are battle-tested before final placements.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 2-year MBA program is intense and fast-paced. Semester 1 & 2 build a robust foundation across all business verticals. Between Year 1 and 2, students undergo a strict Summer Internship Program (SIP). Semesters 3 & 4 are heavily customized based on the student's chosen specialization (Marketing, HR, or Finance).

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Principles of Management & OB, Managerial Economics, Accounting for Managers, Quantitative Techniques for Business, Business Communication, IT for Managers. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Financial Management, Marketing Management, Human Resource Management, Research Methodology, Operations Management, Corporate Law. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Strategic Management, Specialization Major I, Specialization Major II, Specialization Major III, Specialization Minor I, Summer Internship Report & Viva. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Business Ethics & Corporate Governance, Specialization Major IV, Specialization Major V, Specialization Minor II, Comprehensive Capstone Project. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Marketing Specialization Subjects
accordionSections.content: Consumer Behavior, Digital & Social Media Marketing, Brand Management, Retail & Supply Chain, International Marketing.

accordionSections.title: Finance Specialization Subjects
accordionSections.content: Investment Banking, Financial Derivatives, Security Analysis & Portfolio Management, Corporate Taxation, Mergers & Acquisitions.

accordionSections.title: HR Specialization Subjects
accordionSections.content: Talent Acquisition & Retention, Performance Management, Labor Laws & Industrial Relations, Organizational Development, HR Analytics.

admissionFee.subtitle: Transparent fee structure with high ROI

admissionCriteria.text: Admission through CPU Entrance Exam / CAT / MAT / CMAT scores, followed by Group Discussion & Personal Interview (GD-PI).

notes: Scholarships are evaluated based on National Level Entrance scores (CAT/MAT) or Graduation percentage.
Tuition and fees may be net once your scholarship criteria is evaluated.
Special scholarships exist for students with prior corporate work experience (1+ years).
If a student fails to maintain the required CGPA, they may lose scholarship eligibility.
scholarships.subtitle: Scholarship for Session 2026-27

reasons.desc: We move away from rote learning. Our pedagogy relies on real-time Harvard and IIM case studies to develop critical business thinking.
reasons.title: Case-Study Methodology

reasons.desc: Our dedicated corporate relations team ensures our MBA graduates consistently secure placements in top-tier MNCs and Big 4 consulting firms.
reasons.title: Elite Corporate Placements

reasons.desc: Choose between HR, Marketing, or Finance to build deep, specialized knowledge that makes you a subject matter expert in the corporate world.
reasons.title: Deep Specialization

reasons.desc: A mandatory 8-week Summer Internship Program (SIP) with leading companies ensures you have actual corporate experience before you graduate.
reasons.title: Mandatory Summer Internship

items.a: Candidates must have completed their graduation (B.A., B.Sc., B.Com, B.Tech, etc.) from a recognized university with a minimum of 50% marks. Final year appearing students can also apply.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for MBA admission?

items.a: Yes, we accept valid scores from national-level entrance exams like CAT, MAT, XAT, and CMAT. Students with high scores are directly shortlisted for the GD-PI round and are eligible for specific scholarships.
items.q: Do you accept CAT/MAT scores?

items.a: We offer specialized tracks in three major areas: Human Resource Management (HR), Marketing Management, and Financial Management. Students select their major and minor in the second year.
items.q: What specializations are available in this MBA?

items.a: Our MBA graduates are aggressively recruited by top MNCs, banks, and consulting firms. Roles include Investment Bankers, Brand Managers, HR Business Partners, and Financial Analysts. The highest package recently recorded was 24 LPA.
items.q: What kind of placements can I expect?

items.a: Yes, an 8-week Summer Internship Program (SIP) between the first and second year is strictly mandatory. The university's placement cell actively assists in securing these corporate internships.
items.q: Is the Summer Internship compulsory?

items.a: Absolutely. Prior work experience brings valuable context to classroom discussions. We often see professionals with 1-3 years of experience joining the program to accelerate their career into mid-management roles.
items.q: Is work experience required? Will it help?

items.a: MBA students have access to specialized simulation labs, executive boardrooms for mock meetings, a dedicated incubation center for startups, and a comprehensive digital management library.
items.q: What facilities do MBA students get access to?

mba-banking-finance.title: MBA in Banking & Finance
mba-banking-finance.eligibility: Graduation in any discipline with minimum 50% marks from a recognized university
mba-banking-finance.description: An elite postgraduate specialization designed to forge financial leaders, investment bankers, and wealth managers equipped to navigate the dynamic global financial sector.
mba-banking-finance.metaTitle: MBA Banking & Finance | Commerce & Management | CPU
mba-banking-finance.metaDescription: Pursue a 2-year MBA in Banking & Finance at Career Point University. Master investment banking, risk management, and fintech. Secure top corporate finance placements.

hero.description: Master the complexities of global capital markets. Build expertise in corporate finance, investment banking, and the rapidly evolving FinTech landscape.

overview.subtitle: What is the MBA Banking & Finance Programme?
paragraphs: The MBA in Banking & Finance is a specialized premium program that bridges traditional corporate finance with modern financial technology (FinTech). It is designed to prepare students for the high-stakes, fast-paced world of banking and capital markets.

Through live stock market trading simulations, corporate financial modeling, and strict alignment with regulatory certifications (like NISM and NCFM), graduates step into the industry not as trainees, but as highly skilled financial professionals ready to drive revenue.

scope.body: The financial sector is the backbone of the global economy. Graduates of this specialization are aggressively recruited by Private Banks, Big 4 Audit Firms (EY, KPMG, PwC, Deloitte), Asset Management Companies (AMCs), and FinTech startups. Highly lucrative career roles include Investment Banker, Risk Analyst, Wealth Manager, Credit Manager, and Financial Planner. This degree also provides the perfect analytical base for those wishing to clear the prestigious CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst) exams.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in global capital markets

curriculum.subtitle: An elite curriculum designed for financial dominance
curriculum.introNote: The curriculum is split: Year 1 establishes core management principles, while Year 2 dives exclusively into advanced finance, banking operations, and quantitative modeling.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory 8-week internship in a banking or financial institution ensures real-world corporate finance exposure.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 2-year MBA (Banking & Finance) program is intense and fast-paced. Semester 1 & 2 build a robust foundation across general business management with a tilt towards accounting. Semesters 3 & 4 focus entirely on the banking sector, derivatives, portfolio management, and financial technology.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Principles of Management, Managerial Economics, Financial Accounting for Managers, Quantitative Techniques, Business Communication, Financial Markets & Institutions. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Corporate Financial Management, Banking Operations & Management, Human Resource Management, Marketing of Financial Services, Operations Research, Business Law. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Security Analysis & Portfolio Management, International Financial Management, Credit Appraisal & Risk Management in Banking, FinTech & Digital Banking, Financial Modeling, Summer Internship Report. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Financial Derivatives, Wealth Management & Mutual Funds, Investment Banking & Financial Services, Business Ethics & Corporate Governance, Comprehensive Capstone Project. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Departmental Elective (Value Adds)
accordionSections.content: Students must complete mandatory certifications in NISM (Mutual Funds/Securities) and NCFM modules during their coursework.

admissionFee.subtitle: Transparent fee structure with high ROI

admissionCriteria.text: Admission through CPU Entrance Exam / CAT / MAT / CMAT scores, followed by Group Discussion & Personal Interview (GD-PI).

notes: Scholarships are evaluated based on National Level Entrance scores (CAT/MAT) or Graduation percentage.
Tuition and fees may be net once your scholarship criteria is evaluated.
Special scholarships exist for students with prior banking/corporate work experience (1+ years).
If a student fails to maintain the required CGPA, they may lose scholarship eligibility.

reasons.title: Elite Bank Placements

reasons.desc: A mandatory 8-week Summer Internship Program (SIP) with a recognized bank or NBFC ensures you have actual financial sector experience.

items.a: Candidates must have completed their graduation from a recognized university with a minimum of 50% marks. While a B.Com or BBA background is helpful, students from Engineering (B.Tech) and Science streams are highly encouraged to apply for the quantitative rigor.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria? Can non-commerce students apply?

items.a: Yes, we accept valid scores from national-level entrance exams like CAT, MAT, XAT, and CMAT. Students with high scores are directly shortlisted for the GD-PI round and are eligible for specific scholarships.
items.q: Do you accept CAT/MAT scores?

items.a: A general MBA Finance covers broader corporate finance. The MBA in Banking & Finance is hyper-specialized, focusing deeply on banking operations, forex, investment banking, risk management, and the modern FinTech ecosystem.
items.q: How is this different from a general MBA in Finance?

items.a: Our graduates are recruited by Private Banks (HDFC, ICICI, Axis), Big 4 Firms (KPMG, Deloitte), NBFCs, and FinTech companies. Roles include Investment Analysts, Credit Managers, and Wealth Advisors. The highest package recorded was 20+ LPA.
items.q: What kind of placements can I expect?

items.a: Yes, an 8-week Summer Internship Program (SIP) between the first and second year is strictly mandatory. The university's placement cell actively assists in securing these internships in banks or AMCs.
items.q: Is the Summer Internship compulsory?

items.a: Absolutely. Prior work experience in the banking or financial sector brings valuable context to classroom discussions and often helps in securing lateral placements with higher packages.
items.q: Is work experience required? Will it help?

items.a: MBA Banking students have access to a specialized Financial Trading Lab for live market simulations, executive boardrooms for mock pitches, and a comprehensive digital finance library (including Bloomberg data access).

The second year dives exclusively into advanced finance: derivatives, portfolio management, risk assessment, and FinTech, culminating in placements.

pgdm.title: Post Graduate Diploma in Management (PGDM)
pgdm.eligibility: Graduation in any discipline with minimum 50% marks from a recognized university
pgdm.description: An agile, highly practical, and industry-integrated management program designed to bridge the gap between academic theory and real-world corporate execution.
pgdm.metaTitle: PGDM Course | Post Graduate Diploma in Management | CPU
pgdm.metaDescription: Enroll in the 2-year PGDM program at Career Point University. Experience a highly practical, industry-aligned curriculum with top MNC placement assistance.

hero.description: Accelerate your career with a corporate-driven curriculum. Learn directly from industry experts, solve live business problems, and secure top-tier management roles.

overview.subtitle: What is the PGDM Programme?
paragraphs: The Post Graduate Diploma in Management (PGDM) is designed for students who want to hit the ground running. Unlike traditional degrees, the PGDM curriculum is continuously updated by an Industry Advisory Board to reflect the latest trends in the global market.

Our pedagogy heavily emphasizes experiential learning, corporate bootcamps, out-bound leadership training, and live industry projects. This ensures that PGDM graduates are not just academically sound, but practically equipped to handle high-pressure corporate roles from day one.

scope.body: Because of its practical nature, the PGDM is highly preferred by modern, agile companies including top consulting firms, FMCG giants, E-Commerce startups, and IT conglomerates. Graduates are aggressively recruited for roles such as Operations Manager, Supply Chain Analyst, Digital Marketing Head, Financial Consultant, and Business Development Manager. The rigorous soft-skills and leadership training also builds a strong foundation for future entrepreneurs.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in a fast-paced corporate world

curriculum.subtitle: An agile curriculum designed by industry leaders
curriculum.introNote: The PGDM curriculum focuses heavily on current business tools, analytical thinking, and immediate corporate execution rather than just theory.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory, intensive Summer Internship Program (SIP) and a live Capstone Project ensure absolute industry readiness.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 2-year PGDM program is highly immersive. The first year builds cross-functional business acumen. The second year allows students to pursue Dual Specializations, giving them a competitive edge in multi-disciplinary corporate roles.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Organizational Behavior, Managerial Economics, Financial Accounting, Marketing Management, Quantitative Methods, Business Communication Lab. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Corporate Finance, Human Resource Management, Operations & Supply Chain, Research Methodology, Business Analytics, Design Thinking & Innovation. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Strategic Management, Specialization I (Subject 1 & 2), Specialization II (Subject 1 & 2), Corporate Leadership Bootcamp, Summer Internship Report. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Business Ethics & Law, Specialization I (Subject 3), Specialization II (Subject 3), Global Business Environment, Final Capstone Industry Project. Total Credits: 24

accordionSections.title: Dual Specialization Options
accordionSections.content: Students can choose two majors from: Marketing, Finance, Human Resources, Supply Chain & Logistics, Business Analytics, and International Business.

admissionFee.subtitle: Transparent fee structure with high ROI

admissionCriteria.text: Admission through CPU Entrance Exam / CAT / MAT / CMAT scores, followed by Group Discussion & Personal Interview (GD-PI).

notes: Scholarships are evaluated based on National Level Entrance scores (CAT/MAT) or Graduation percentage.
Tuition and fees may be net once your scholarship criteria is evaluated.
Special scholarships exist for students with prior corporate work experience (1+ years).
If a student fails to maintain the required CGPA, they may lose scholarship eligibility.

reasons.title: Updated, Agile Curriculum

items.a: Candidates must have completed their graduation from a recognized university with a minimum of 50% marks. Final year appearing students can also apply.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for PGDM admission?

items.a: An MBA is a traditional university degree with a fixed syllabus. A PGDM is an autonomous diploma where the syllabus is highly flexible and updated regularly by industry experts to meet current corporate demands. Employers highly value PGDM for its practical approach.
items.q: What is the difference between an MBA and a PGDM?

items.a: Yes, we accept valid scores from national-level entrance exams like CAT, MAT, XAT, and CMAT. Good scores give you an edge in the GD-PI rounds and make you eligible for scholarships.
items.q: Do you accept CAT/MAT scores?

items.a: PGDM graduates are aggressively recruited by top consulting firms, FMCG brands, Banks, and Tech companies. Roles include Business Analysts, Marketing Managers, and Financial Consultants. The highest package recorded was 18+ LPA.
items.q: What kind of placements can I expect?

items.a: Yes, an 8-10 week Summer Internship Program (SIP) is strictly mandatory. Our corporate relations team ensures students are placed in reputed organizations for this training.
items.q: Is the Summer Internship compulsory?

items.a: We offer dual specializations. You can choose two fields from Marketing, Human Resources, Financial Services, Supply Chain Management, Business Analytics, and International Business.
items.q: What specializations are available in PGDM?

items.a: Absolutely. Prior work experience brings valuable context to case-study discussions. Many professionals join the PGDM program to upskill and accelerate their career growth.

years.contentDesc: The first year builds a rock-solid, practical foundation across all business verticals, ending with an immersive 8-week corporate summer internship.

mha.title: Master of Hospital Administration (MHA)
mha.eligibility: Graduation in any discipline (Medical/Life Sciences/BHA preferred) with minimum 50% marks
mha.description: An advanced postgraduate degree designed to create visionary healthcare leaders capable of managing complex operations in multi-specialty hospitals and healthcare organizations.
mha.metaTitle: Master of Hospital Administration (MHA) | Healthcare Management | CPU
mha.metaDescription: Pursue a 2-year MHA degree at Career Point University. Master hospital operations, NABH quality standards, and health economics. Secure top roles in corporate hospitals.

hero.description: Lead the future of healthcare. Master hospital operations, clinical quality management, and medical laws through rigorous internships and live hospital projects.

overview.subtitle: What is the MHA Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Hospital Administration (MHA) is a highly specialized program that bridges the gap between medical science and corporate management. Running a modern hospital requires more than medical expertise; it demands rigorous operational planning, financial management, and strict adherence to quality laws.

This program trains students to manage the entire lifecycle of a hospital—from the architectural planning of clinical facilities to the daily operations of emergency wards, material management, and handling health insurance (TPA) claims. It is the gold standard degree for anyone aiming for the C-Suite in the healthcare sector.

scope.body: The healthcare industry is experiencing unprecedented growth, making Hospital Administration one of the most recession-proof careers globally. MHA graduates are aggressively hired by Corporate Hospitals (Apollo, Fortis, Medanta), Healthcare Consultancies, Health Insurance Firms, and Medical Tourism companies. Key roles include Medical Superintendent, Chief Operating Officer (COO), Quality Manager (NABH), and Healthcare HR Director.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in the global healthcare ecosystem

curriculum.subtitle: An elite curriculum designed for healthcare leadership
curriculum.introNote: The MHA curriculum provides a deep dive into medical terminology, healthcare laws, and advanced management principles tailored specifically for the medical sector.
curriculum.outroNote: A mandatory 12-week intensive hospital residency and a final healthcare thesis ensure absolute operational readiness.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The 2-year MHA program is highly immersive. The first year focuses on understanding the healthcare delivery system, medical terminology, and basic hospital operations. The second year delves into advanced topics like medical tourism, disaster management, quality accreditations, and long-term hospital residencies.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Principles of Management in Healthcare, Epidemiology & Public Health, Medical Terminology & Anatomy, Health Economics, Financial Accounting for Hospitals, Healthcare Communication. Total Credits: 26

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Hospital Operations Management (Clinical & Diagnostic), Human Resource Management in Hospitals, Healthcare Laws & Medical Ethics, Marketing of Healthcare Services, Research Methodology. Total Credits: 26

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Quality Management & Patient Safety (NABH/JCI), Hospital Planning & Architecture, Material & Supply Chain Management, Health Insurance & TPA Operations, Hospital Information Systems (HIS). Total Credits: 26

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Strategic Management in Healthcare, Medical Tourism, Disaster Management in Hospitals, 12-Week Hospital Residency (Internship), Comprehensive Healthcare Thesis & Viva-Voce. Total Credits: 26

accordionSections.title: Departmental Elective (Value Adds)
accordionSections.content: Students undergo specialized workshops on Basic Life Support (BLS), Bio-Medical Waste Management, and Healthcare Analytics.

admissionFee.subtitle: Transparent fee structure with high ROI

admissionCriteria.text: Admission through CPU Entrance Exam / CAT / MAT / CMAT scores, followed by Group Discussion & Personal Interview (GD-PI).

notes: Special flat scholarships are available for students holding medical degrees (MBBS, BDS, BAMS) moving into administration.
Scholarships are evaluated based on Entrance scores or Graduation percentage.
Tuition and fees may be net once your scholarship criteria is evaluated.
If a student fails to maintain the required CGPA, they may lose scholarship eligibility.
scholarships.subtitle: Scholarship for Session 2026-27

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the MHA programme

items.a: Graduates from any discipline with 50% marks can apply. However, candidates with a background in Life Sciences, Medicine (MBBS, BDS, BAMS, BHMS), Pharmacy, Nursing, or a BHA degree are highly preferred and excel quickly in this program.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria? Can non-medical students apply?

items.a: A general MBA focuses on generic corporate business models. An MHA is hyper-specialized; it replaces generic subjects with Hospital Planning, Healthcare Laws, Medical Terminology, Epidemiology, and NABH Quality standards.
items.q: How is MHA different from a general MBA?

items.a: Yes, we accept valid scores from national-level entrance exams like CAT, MAT, XAT. Alternatively, students can appear for the CPU internal entrance test.
items.q: Do I need to clear an entrance exam?

items.a: MHA graduates are hired as Hospital Operations Managers, Quality Control Coordinators, TPA/Insurance Heads, and Facility Directors in hospitals like Apollo, Fortis, and Max Healthcare.
items.q: What kind of placements can I expect?

items.a: Yes, a 12-week Hospital Residency (Internship) is strictly mandatory in the final semester. Students are placed in functioning hospitals to handle real administrative duties before graduating.
items.q: Is a hospital internship compulsory?

items.a: No. The MHA is an administrative and management degree. You will be managing the operations of the hospital, the doctors, and the facilities, but you cannot prescribe medicine or treat patients.
items.q: Will I be able to treat patients after doing an MHA?

items.a: MHA students have access to Hospital Information System (HIS) software labs, healthcare case study libraries, and mock hospital layout simulation rooms.
items.q: What facilities do MHA students get access to?

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the MHA programme

roadmap.subtitle: Your journey from graduate to hospital administrator.

years.contentDesc: The first year builds a robust foundation in management principles while introducing critical medical terminologies, epidemiology, and health economics.
The second year dives exclusively into healthcare laws, NABH quality accreditations, and culminates in a 12-week intensive hospital residency.

list.name: Dr. Ananya Desai
list.text: Moving from a clinical background (BDS) to administration was daunting, but the MHA program bridged that gap perfectly. The 12-week residency secured my current role.

list.name: Saurabh Mehra
list.text: The deep focus on NABH, patient safety, and medical laws in the final year directly aligned with my career goals. The faculty's industry experience is unmatched.

list.name: Rahul Verma
list.text: Learning the intricacies of Health IT and Hospital Information Systems gave me a massive edge in the healthcare insurance sector.

title.main: Business Leaders Making a Global Impact

community.label: Community
description: The School of Commerce & Management is more than just classrooms; it is a vibrant corporate ecosystem. Our student body is a diverse mix of aspiring entrepreneurs, financial wizards, and healthcare administrators, all collaborating to build the next big idea.

Through our active Management Club, Finance Society, and Startup Incubation center, students engage in live stock market trading simulations, business plan pitch-fests, and high-stakes corporate debates, preparing them for the realities of the business world.

gallery.caption: Students pitching at the Annual Startup Fest
